attacking_style: Deploys tactical decoy attacks rather than genuine victory bids, typically launching inside final 2-3km or on climbs to set up teammates. Functions as strategic disruptor rather than primary aggressor.
team_tactics: Sacrificial role - leads out teammate attacks from the bunch or launches diversionary moves to isolate team leaders. Expendable early aggressor who creates tactical chaos for others to exploit.
terrain_preference: Versatile enough for both flat finales and climbing stages, but role is tactical positioning rather than terrain-specific. Most effective where team can leverage numerical advantage.
vulnerability: Not a finishing threat himself - purely a setup rider. Ineffective when team lacks numbers or clear protected rider. Tactical value diminished in solo efforts.
key_patterns: Launches decoy/sacrificial attacks to force rival responses, evidenced across 2 observations spanning 3 years.
Leads out or enables teammate attacks from bunch rather than seeking personal results (2 instances).
Deploys tactical moves in critical moments: inside 2km finale or on key climbs (2 observations).
Functions as tactical disruptor across both Grand Tours and week-long stage races.
form_trajectory: neutral: Role consistency maintained across 2020-2023 observations, suggesting stable tactical function rather than performance evolution
